title: Curriculum Design: Special Programs
description: Conroe ISD's approach to designing and delivering special academic programs — such as gifted and talented, advanced coursework, and other targeted instructional offerings — is guided by this policy. It outlines how the district identifies, develops, and supports these programs to meet the diverse learning needs of students. Parents and community members can use this policy to understand how specialized educational opportunities are structured and made available across the district.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's EHB (Local) policy on Curriculum Design: Special Programs streamlines the dyslexia compliance language by removing the reference to State Board of Education standards and commissioner guidance, and adds an explicit reference to the current version of The Dyslexia Handbook as the governing resource.
changes_added: This includes the current version of The Dyslexia Handbook.
changes_removed: and standards adopted by the State Board of Education and guidance published by the commissioner of education to implement the program to test
timeline_local.notes: The redline on page 2 shows red strikethrough and red underline text clearly. The removed text replaces a lengthy phrase about State Board of Education standards and commissioner guidance, and the word 'for' is removed after 'with'. The added text substitutes 'regarding' and appends a new sentence referencing The Dyslexia Handbook. Cover page title reads 'Discuss and Possibly Adopt Revisions to Board Policy EHB (Local)', mapped to action 'adopted'.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Policy EHB (Local) streamlines the compliance language for dyslexia and related disorders by replacing a detailed reference to specific State Board of Education rules, standards, and commissioner guidance with a broader reference to all state requirements, and explicitly adds a reference to the current version of The Dyslexia Handbook.
changes_added: This includes the current version of The Dyslexia Handbook.
changes_removed: rules and standards adopted by the State Board of Education and guidance published by the commissioner of education to implement the program to test
timeline_local.notes: The redline replaces "rules and standards adopted by the State Board of Education and guidance published by the commissioner of education to implement the program to test" (red strikethrough) with "regarding" (red underlined), and replaces "for" with "with for" (red underlined addition of "with"), and adds the new sentence "This includes the current version of The Dyslexia Handbook." The word "state" appears to be underlined in red as well, indicating it was retained/re-added as part of the revision. Cover page title heading reads "Discuss Revisions to Board Policy EHB (Local)" → first_reading.

description_local: This policy governs how schools design and deliver special academic programs, with a particular focus on supporting students with dyslexia and related reading difficulties. It applies to students who may need specialized instruction, as well as the educators and administrators responsible for providing it. Parents should be aware of this policy because it outlines the standards schools must follow to identify and support children with dyslexia, helping ensure those students receive appropriate help in the classroom.
